blurb: Gritters Library is hosting a free one-hour lunch-and-learn on Thursday at noon focused on staying safe online in the age of AI, and this one is actually worth your hour. Scams are getting smarter. Phishing emails look more legitimate than ever. And if you've got aging parents or teenagers in the house, the threats hitting their inboxes look nothing like what you were trained to spot five years ago. The Cobb County B.E.S.T Learning Center is running the session, it's free, it's an hour, and it's right here in East Cobb. Bring a laptop if you want to follow along.
scoring_notes: Solid fit for East Cobb's educated, tech-aware demographic. The AI angle makes it timely and relevant. Lower uniqueness score because library programs like this recur, but the specific AI cybersecurity topic makes this a cut above a generic computer class. Good for the empty nester segment especially, and parents who are thinking about their kids' online safety.

event_name: Intro to Fishing: Spinning Reels at SITKA Gear
blurb: If your fishing setup is still stuck in Zebco territory, SITKA Gear at The Battery is hosting a free two-hour clinic on Wednesday night to help you actually understand how a spinning reel works. Not a sales pitch, a real hands-on intro covering functionality, technique, and how to step your game up on the water. It's a weeknight, it's free, and it's at The Battery, so you can grab dinner before or after. Good for beginners who want to get into fishing, and honestly useful for anyone who's been doing it casually for years without ever learning the mechanics properly.
scoring_notes: Niche but genuinely useful for the outdoor-activity-inclined portion of East Cobb's demographic. The $118K median household income suggests a lot of people here own boats or fish recreationally. Free, accessible, and educational. Lower scores across the board because it's narrower in appeal than the other events, but it's a real event with a clear value proposition for the right reader.

event_name: Atlanta Braves vs New York Mets
blurb: The Mets come to Truist Park on July 3rd for a Friday night game, which means you're picking between fireworks in a parking lot somewhere or watching the Braves play under the lights on the night before the Fourth. That's not a hard call. The Battery will be buzzing all evening, and a 7:15 first pitch gives you plenty of time to grab dinner before the game. It's a holiday weekend, the weather is warm, and baseball is exactly what summer is supposed to feel like. Get your tickets early, this one will sell.
scoring_notes: Second Braves game in the window, same venue, slightly lower uniqueness since there's no giveaway noted and it's the same setup as candidate 3. Still strong demographic fit and a great holiday-weekend option, especially for those who can't make Thursday. Ranked lower than the Cardinals game due to the cap giveaway differentiator being absent.

blurb: After the cookouts and the fireworks and the family chaos of the Fourth, Sunday night is for something a little more fun. Springs Cinema & Taphouse is screening Jaws on July 5th, which is about as perfect a summer movie as exists, and doing it at a taphouse means you can grab a drink and watch a great white terrorize Amity Island from a real seat with real food in front of you. Roy Scheider. Robert Shaw. Richard Dreyfuss. "You're gonna need a bigger boat." If you haven't seen it on a big screen, this is the one. It's a CLASSIC, and these special screenings don't come around all the time.
scoring_notes: Special screening of a classic film at a dine-in taphouse is a strong fit for the 42-median-age East Cobb audience who grew up with Jaws. Adults and older teens will connect with this one. Good date night or post-holiday-weekend activity. Uniqueness score is solid because special theatrical screenings of classic films are genuinely limited-run events.

blurb: Downtown Kennesaw throws one of the better Fourth of July celebrations in the area, and it lands on Friday the 3rd this year, which means you've got a full holiday weekend night to work with. Six hours of live music, festivities, and that classic small-town Independence Day energy that honestly never gets old. It's free, it's close, and it's the kind of evening where you show up at 5, grab food, find a good spot, and let the night do its thing. If you've got family in town for the holiday, this is a solid anchor for the evening.
scoring_notes: Annual July 4th celebration in a nearby community. Free admission and proximity make it highly accessible for East Cobb families. Slightly lower uniqueness score because it's a recurring annual event, but the holiday context and community energy still make it genuinely worthwhile to promote. Good fit for families with teens and multigenerational groups.

blurb: This one is worth knowing about if you want more than just a game. Bats & Bites gets you into Truist Park early for a guided behind-the-scenes tour that ends in the Outfield Market, where you'll sample new food items, meet a former Braves player, and watch batting practice from the outfield. It's a real look at what goes on before the gates open, which most fans never get to see. Runs from 3 to 7pm on July 3rd, so it slots right before the evening Mets game if you want to make a full day of it at The Battery. Spots are limited, so check availability now.
scoring_notes: High uniqueness score because behind-the-scenes stadium access plus player interaction is genuinely rare and not something you can do any weekend. Strong for Braves fans of any age, great for families with teen kids who are into baseball. Slightly lower audience match than the straight game because it requires more deliberate planning and likely costs more.

event_name: Atlanta Braves vs St. Louis Cardinals
blurb: The holiday weekend kicks off Thursday night with the Braves hosting the Cardinals at Truist Park, and this one comes with a bonus: it's a Corduroy Throwback Cap Giveaway night. If you've been looking for a reason to finally make it out to a game this summer, a free hat and a 7:15 first pitch on the unofficial start of the Fourth of July stretch is about as good a setup as you're going to get. Truist Park is a 15-minute drive from most of East Cobb, which means no excuse not to go. Grab tickets before they move.
scoring_notes: Braves games are a perennial favorite for East Cobb households at this income level. The throwback cap giveaway adds a uniqueness bump to what would otherwise be a standard game. Strong for both families with teens and couples doing a date night. Proximity to East Cobb is a genuine selling point worth calling out.

blurb: This is how you spend the Fourth. The Braves are hosting a full-on block party at The Battery before the game, with live entertainment, nonstop energy, and that whole electric pre-game atmosphere that makes The Battery one of the best spots in Atlanta on a holiday weekend. It runs from 4 to 8pm, so you've got time to eat, walk around, and soak it in before first pitch. Bring the family, bring the neighbors, bring whoever you've been meaning to make plans with all summer. No admission, right in your backyard, and it's the Fourth of July. That combo doesn't come around every weekend.
scoring_notes: July 4th Block Party at The Battery hits every note for East Cobb families and empty nesters alike. The Battery is already a go-to destination for this demographic. The holiday timing makes this a special-occasion version of a familiar spot. Free admission removes any friction. Strong family and adult crossover appeal.
